title: Sanitizers, Static Analysis, and Fuzzing · Mastery Check
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar sanitizers, static analysis, and fuzzing decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for layered memory-defect test matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fully instrumented MemorySanitizer environments for their distinct defect classes.
Run Clang and GCC static analyzers, preserve paths and assumptions, classify findings, and regression-test repairs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Asan Lsan Evidence · changed case · Quiz Asan Lsan Evidence
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 9791. Produce bounded C evidence to classify a defect against complementary native tools; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.
